titleStmt.title: SII 38.300: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 300. (A.R. No. 300 of 1912.) ATTI, CHEYYAR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the west wall, Agastīśvara temple.

summary.p: It begins with the name (of a chief ?) Aḻagiyachōḻaṉ. It records that the paḷḷans of Atti and Āḷappirandān agreedIt may also be construed that the paḷḷans of both the above villages declared among themselves that their lands (bhūmi) were tax-free (saggēttam) since the beginning.among themselves (the nature of the agreement is not known) and those who violated this agreement would incur the sin of killing a tawny cow on the bank of the river Ganges.
This record on the grounds of palaeography may be assigned to the 17th century A.D.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
Aḻakiya coḻaṉ
piṟapavaruṣam cittiraimātam 5 Atti Āḷappiṟantān yinta yiraṇṭu Ūrukkum paḷḷakaḷ caṅkettam pū(m)mi toṇiṉa
ṉāḷ mutal cantirātittavaraikkum yillai yitukku Ārākilum yiraṇṭu niṉcālRead niṉaittālkeṅkaikkaraiyile
kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa toṣattile pokakaṭavaṉ .
Digital edition of SII 38.300 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
